criteria.name: Awards
criteria.evidence: Three IFSC World Cup podium finishes (2 silver, 1 bronze); US national champion 2022
criteria.rationale: International podium finishes at IFSC World Cup level and a national championship title constitute nationally and internationally recognized prizes in the field.

criteria.name: Membership
criteria.evidence: Member of US national team (implied by head coach role and competitive history)
criteria.rationale: National team membership may qualify but no explicit description of membership in associations requiring outstanding achievement was provided.

criteria.name: Press
criteria.rationale: No press, media features, or published coverage about the applicant was mentioned in the background.

criteria.name: Judging
criteria.rationale: No participation as a judge, panelist, or evaluator of others' work in the field was mentioned.

criteria.name: Original contribution
criteria.evidence: Head coach of US national youth climbing team; multi-year sponsorship deals with major outdoor brands
criteria.rationale: Coaching at the national team level may support an original methodological or training contribution, but no specific innovation or documented impact was described.

criteria.name: Scholarly articles
criteria.rationale: The background explicitly states no academic publications exist.

criteria.name: Critical role
criteria.evidence: Head coach of the US national youth climbing team
criteria.rationale: A head coach position for a national team is a distinguishing, critical role within a distinguished organization in the field.

criteria.name: High remuneration
criteria.evidence: Multi-year sponsorship deals with two major outdoor brands
criteria.rationale: Sponsorship deals with major brands suggest above-average remuneration, but no figures were provided to compare against peers.

gaps: Provide documentation of any press, magazine features, or media coverage about Marcus Bell specifically (not just event results)
Document any judging, evaluation, or selection-committee roles at competitions or team trials
Quantify sponsorship compensation with figures and compare to peer athletes to support high remuneration
Identify and document membership in elite climbing associations or governing bodies requiring outstanding achievement for admission
Articulate any specific training methodology or coaching innovation attributable to the applicant to strengthen original contribution
Gather letters from national federation officials or brand partners attesting to the critical and distinguished nature of the coaching role
